title: Abandonment of servient estate.
doc_html:
Art. 770.  Abandonment of servient estate.

A predial servitude is extinguished by the abandonment of the servient estate, or of the part on which the servitude is exercised.  It must be evidenced by a written act.  The owner of the dominant estate is bound to accept it and confusion takes place.

Acts 1977, No. 514, §1.
